evals.prompt: /acceptance for the webhook-retry epic. verifier passed everything. breaker came back with just 'No findings — the implementation looks correct.' but didn't actually analyze any of the 5 angles
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the breaker report is structurally invalid — 'no findings' without per-angle analysis (implied requirements, edge cases, integration points, technically-met-but-incomplete, missing negatives). Re-runs the breaker with explicit instruction to analyze all 5 angles. Does NOT auto-pass based on a rubber-stamp breaker report.
expectations: Detects bare 'no findings' without per-angle analysis is invalid per breaker requirements.
Re-runs breaker with explicit instruction to analyze all 5 angles with concrete observations.
Does NOT auto-pass — a lazy breaker defeats the purpose of dual-agent verification.

evals.prompt: /acceptance for task #42 (rate limiting handler). verifier says all 3 criteria PASS with evidence. breaker analyzed all 5 angles and found one HIGH: 'criterion 2 (handle rate limiting) is technically met — code catches 429 errors — but silently drops the request instead of retrying. the criterion says handle, not just catch.'
evals.expected_output: Applies reconciliation rule 3: Verifier PASS + breaker HIGH on a PASS criterion = PARTIAL. The breaker caught a real gap the verifier missed — catching 429 without retrying doesn't fully satisfy 'handle rate limiting'. Shows both reports with clear labels. Presents 4 options via AskUserQuestion: fix gaps, commit anyway, file as follow-up, re-run after manual fixes.
expectations: Verdict is PARTIAL (rule 3: verifier PASS + breaker HIGH on PASS criterion). Not PASS despite verifier's assessment.
Shows both verifier and breaker reports — user needs to see the disagreement.
Presents exactly 4 options: fix gaps, commit anyway (override), file as follow-up tasks, re-run after manual fixes.

evals.prompt: /acceptance --auto for the auth-middleware epic. verifier found criterion 3 FAIL (JWT expiry not checked). breaker found a HIGH on criterion 1 (session validation accepts expired tokens). first fix attempt didn't improve anything
evals.expected_output: With --auto and FAIL verdict, automatically tries to fix gaps (option 1). After first fix attempt with no improvement, tries once more (2 fix iterations allowed). If second attempt also fails, automatically overrides and commits anyway (option 2). Notes the override in findings.
expectations: With --auto, FAIL triggers automatic fix attempt — no AskUserQuestion.
After 2 fix iterations with no improvement, falls back to commit anyway (override). Notes override in findings.
Does NOT loop indefinitely — 2 fix attempts max before override.

evals.prompt:
/acceptance

no task id, just check whatever epic is most recent. i think it was the webhook retry one
evals.expected_output: No argument provided. TaskList filtered by type='epic' and status in ['completed', 'in_progress']. Picks highest ID (most recent). Enters epic mode — recursively collects all descendants, groups criteria by subtree (phases as section headers, leaves with acceptance criteria). Spawns verifier, breaker, and test auditor in parallel against the diff.
expectations: Resolves target via TaskList without argument — picks highest-ID epic with status completed/in_progress.
Epic mode: recursively collects descendants, groups criteria by subtree.
Spawns verifier, breaker, and test auditor as parallel agents.

evals.prompt: /acceptance for epic 200. one of the phase tasks (status=completed) has a child task still pending — looks like it got orphaned when the parent was marked complete
evals.expected_output: Detects the orphaned task during criteria gathering (pending child with completed parent). Issues an advisory warning but does NOT block acceptance. Continues with normal verification flow for all descendants.
expectations: Flags the orphaned task (pending child, completed parent) with a warning.
Warning is advisory only — does NOT block acceptance.
Continues normal verification flow for all other descendants.

evals.prompt: /acceptance for the auth-service epic. verifier passed all criteria. breaker analyzed all 5 angles, found one MEDIUM (session token rotation not tested under concurrent requests). test auditor came back with 'SUFFICIENT — all criteria are well covered by tests' but didn't actually list which tests cover which criteria
evals.expected_output: Detects the auditor report is structurally invalid — 'SUFFICIENT' without per-criterion test mapping. Re-runs the auditor with explicit instruction to map specific test names to each criterion. Does NOT accept the rubber-stamp verdict.
expectations: Detects 'SUFFICIENT' without per-criterion analysis is structurally invalid — same rubber-stamp guard as Breaker.
Re-runs auditor with explicit instruction to show per-criterion test mapping.
Does NOT accept the verdict — a lazy auditor defeats the purpose of test sufficiency checking.

evals.prompt: /acceptance for the payment-gateway epic. verifier PASS, breaker found one MEDIUM. test auditor found 2 CLEAR gaps: 'no test for 402 Payment Required response' and 'no test for idempotency key collision'. test writer wrote both tests successfully. fresh auditor on re-run returned SUFFICIENT
evals.expected_output: Test sufficiency loop runs because auditor returned GAPS_FOUND. Iteration 1: test writer fills both CLEAR gaps. Fresh auditor returns SUFFICIENT → loop exits after 1 iteration. Verdict improves — GAPS_FOUND no longer contributes to PARTIAL. Report notes test audit convergence.
expectations: Loop triggers because initial auditor returned GAPS_FOUND with CLEAR gaps.
Test writer writes tests for both CLEAR gaps. Fresh auditor returns SUFFICIENT.
Loop exits after 1 iteration — convergence achieved. Verdict improves accordingly.

evals.prompt: /acceptance for the notification-service epic. test auditor found 3 CLEAR gaps. after iteration 1: 1 gap filled, 2 remain. after iteration 2: still 2 gaps (writer couldn't figure out the test setup). iteration 3: same 2 gaps persist
evals.expected_output: Test sufficiency loop runs for all 3 iterations. Each iteration: test writer attempts CLEAR gaps, fresh auditor re-evaluates. After iteration 3 with CLEAR gaps still unresolved → escalation. Flags unresolved gaps in report as warnings. Does NOT attempt a 4th iteration.
expectations: Loop runs exactly 3 iterations — does not exceed the cap.
After iteration 3 with unresolved CLEAR gaps, escalates with warning in report.
Does NOT attempt iteration 4 — 3 is the hard cap.

evals.prompt: /acceptance for the analytics-dashboard epic. test auditor found 1 CLEAR gap (no test for empty dataset rendering) and 2 AMBIGUOUS gaps (unclear what 'graceful degradation' means for chart components, unclear which metrics are business-critical). test writer filled the CLEAR gap. fresh auditor shows only the 2 AMBIGUOUS gaps remain
evals.expected_output: Test sufficiency loop iteration 1: test writer fills the CLEAR gap. Fresh auditor returns GAPS_FOUND but only AMBIGUOUS gaps remain → loop exits (CLEAR gaps resolved). AMBIGUOUS gaps surface as advisory 'Flagged for review' in the report, not as blockers.
expectations: Loop exits after iteration 1 because only AMBIGUOUS gaps remain — all CLEAR gaps resolved.
AMBIGUOUS gaps appear as advisory 'Flagged for review' notes, not blockers.
Does NOT attempt to write tests for AMBIGUOUS gaps — they require domain knowledge.
